description_long: This object is a ground-mounted solar energy infrastructure featuring a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels supported by a metal framework. The panels are elevated above a patterned base platform, which appears to provide both structural support and possibly housing for electrical components. The array consists of multiple dark, glassy solar modules arranged in a single, continuous row, with visible metallic struts anchoring the panels to the base. At one end of the platform, there is an angled enclosure that may serve as an access point or housing for control equipment. The overall design is robust, with a focus on maximizing solar exposure while maintaining stability. The structure is primarily composed of metal and glass, with some plastic or composite elements likely present in the panel frames and electrical housings.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array with a metal support structure and a patterned base platform, featuring a single row of dark photovoltaic panels and an angled equipment enclosure at one end.
description_view_0: Front view showing the patterned base, the edge of the solar panel array, and the 'SLOPE 4°' marking.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the solar panel row, the supporting metal struts, and the angled equipment enclosure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar panels and the base platform, with the enclosure visible at the far end.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the length of the array, support structure, and the enclosure from a different angle.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 4 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with metal (likely aluminum or steel) support struts anchored to a base platform
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 8 solar panels
